title: pelargonium × recurvifolium
intro: Pelargonium × recurvifolium, also known as Red Leaved Geranium, is a hybrid species of the Pelargonium plant family, known for its attractive, deeply divided leaves and clusters of small, pink to red flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Pelargonium × recurvifolium include its ability to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its potential to help manage stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and capsules, and are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
